scenarios.title: Intermittent DNS Resolution Failures
scenarios.incident_summary: Sporadic timeout errors reported across multiple services. No single service appears fully down. Error rates are fluctuating unpredictably. Some requests succeed normally.

ground_truth.root_cause: Intermittent DNS resolution failures caused by a misconfigured ndots setting in the cluster DNS config. Service-to-service calls randomly fail when the DNS resolver times out, causing cascading partial failures.

error_patterns: DNS resolution failed for order-svc.default.svc.cluster.local
Temporary failure in name resolution — retrying
Connection timed out: failed to resolve upstream hostname
getaddrinfo ETIMEOUT order-svc.default.svc.cluster.local
warn_patterns: DNS lookup took 4800ms (threshold: 1000ms)
Upstream health check flapping for order-svc
ndots:5 search path expansion causing excess DNS queries

error_patterns: Failed to connect to payment-svc: Name resolution timeout
DNS SERVFAIL for payment-svc.default.svc.cluster.local
warn_patterns: Retrying RPC to payment-svc after DNS failure (attempt 2/3)

error_patterns: DNS resolution timeout for notification-svc
Connection to notification-svc failed: ETIMEOUT
Failed to resolve smtp.example.com — DNS timeout
warn_patterns: Email delivery delayed due to DNS resolution issues

error_patterns: Elasticsearch cluster node discovery failed — DNS timeout
warn_patterns: Index sync delayed: unable to resolve elasticsearch.default.svc
Token refresh rate spike detected — 3x normal volume
OAuth2 nonce cache size approaching limit

alert_config.description: Sporadic 502/504 errors on multiple endpoints. Error rate fluctuating between 5-18%.

alert_config.description: Order processing failing intermittently. Some requests succeed, others time out.

alert_config.title: Payment Processing Delays

scenarios.title: TLS Certificate Partial Expiry
scenarios.incident_summary: Intermittent SSL handshake failures between internal services. Some connections succeed while others fail with certificate errors. The issue started around 2 hours ago and is gradually getting worse. External HTTPS traffic appears unaffected.

ground_truth.root_cause: An internal TLS certificate for inter-service mTLS communication expired 2 hours ago. Only services that established new connections after expiry are affected — services with long-lived connection pools still have valid cached sessions. As connection pools cycle, more services are failing.

error_patterns: SSL: error:0A000086:SSL routines::certificate verify failed — peer cert expired
TLS handshake failed to order-svc:443 — CERTIFICATE_EXPIRED
Connection refused by upstream — mTLS validation failure
ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR connecting to payment-svc
warn_patterns: Certificate serial CN=internal.svc.cluster expires at 2026-01-15T12:00:00Z — EXPIRED
mTLS connection pool recycling — 60% of cached sessions invalid
Falling back to HTTP for health checks (TLS failed)

error_patterns: javax.net.ssl.SSLHandshakeException: Remote host terminated the handshake
Failed to establish secure connection to payment-svc — cert expired
warn_patterns: TLS session cache hit rate: 40% (normal: 95%) — sessions being invalidated
Retrying connection to payment-svc with fresh TLS handshake

error_patterns: Incoming mTLS connection rejected — client certificate expired
SSL_ERROR_HANDSHAKE_FAILURE from order-svc connection
warn_patterns: Certificate validation failures: 45 in last 5 minutes

error_patterns: Token validation RPC failed — TLS handshake error to token-issuer
GRPC UNAVAILABLE: SSL handshake failed connecting to auth backend
warn_patterns: gRPC channel reconnection triggered — TLS errors on existing channel

error_patterns: Elasticsearch HTTPS connection failed: certificate expired
Cluster communication interrupted — TLS cert validation failure

warn_patterns: Email delivery queue growing: 250 pending (normal: 10) — possible downstream issue
Webhook callback failures increasing — retry queue depth: 180
Client reconnection rate elevated: 3x normal — possible upstream churn

scenarios.title: Configuration Drift Race Condition
scenarios.incident_summary: Orders are being processed but payments are failing for approximately 30% of transactions. The payment-svc appears healthy on the dashboard but customers are reporting failed purchases. No recent deployments. Issues started gradually over the past hour.

ground_truth.root_cause: A configuration drift between the api-gw routing rules and payment-svc caused by an incomplete config rollout. The api-gw config map was updated to route payment callbacks to a new endpoint path, but the payment-svc config was not updated to register the new path. Requests to the new path return 404, but the old path still works for existing sessions.

error_patterns: 404 Not Found: POST /api/v2/payments/callback — route not registered
Webhook verification failed — request path mismatch: expected /api/v1/payments/callback
Payment confirmation timeout for order ORD-84521 — callback never received
Stripe webhook delivery failed: endpoint /api/v2/payments/callback returned 404
warn_patterns: Config version mismatch detected: api_version=v1 but receiving v2 requests
Incoming webhook requests split: 70% /api/v1 (ok), 30% /api/v2 (404)
Payment retry queue growing: 145 pending retries

error_patterns: Upstream payment-svc returned 404 for POST /api/v2/payments/callback
Payment callback routing failed — config map revision: 2026-01-15T12:00
warn_patterns: Route config updated at 12:00 UTC — new path: /api/v2/payments/callback
Backend payment-svc not acknowledging new endpoint path

error_patterns: Payment confirmation not received for order ORD-84521 after 300s
Order stuck in PAYMENT_PENDING state — timeout exceeded
warn_patterns: 30% of orders failing payment confirmation — entering retry loop
Order completion rate dropped from 98% to 68%

error_patterns: Cannot send order confirmation — order ORD-84521 still in PAYMENT_PENDING
warn_patterns: Notification backlog: 89 pending order confirmation emails
Elevated login attempts — users retrying failed purchase flows
Increased UPDATE queries on orders table — status flip-flopping on 30% of records
